questions.context: Points A and B are 8 cm apart. A region R is defined as: within 5 cm of A AND within 6 cm of B.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Describe the locus of points exactly 5 cm from A.
parts.correctAnswer: A circle centred at A with radius 5 cm
workingSteps: The locus of points 5 cm from A is a circle with centre A and radius 5 cm.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Describe the boundary of region R and state whether region R exists (i.e., whether the two circles intersect).
parts.correctAnswer: Region R is the intersection of the two discs. The circles intersect since 5 + 6 = 11 > 8 and |5 − 6| = 1 < 8.

markScheme.desc: Circle centre A radius 5, circle centre B radius 6
Region R is the overlap (intersection) of the two circles
Circles intersect since 1 < 8 < 11 (sum and difference of radii bracket AB)

workingSteps: For intersection: |r₁ − r₂| < AB < r₁ + r₂ → 1 < 8 < 11 ✓
Region R = the lens-shaped intersection of the two discs

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Describe the locus of points equidistant from A and B and state its relationship to AB.
parts.correctAnswer: The perpendicular bisector of AB: passes through midpoint of AB at right angles to AB.

markScheme.desc: Passes through midpoint of AB, at 90° to AB

workingSteps: Points equidistant from A and B lie on the perpendicular bisector of AB.
This is perpendicular to AB and passes through its midpoint at 4 cm from each end.

questions.context: A point P moves so that its distance from a fixed line l is always equal to its distance from a fixed point F not on l.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Name the curve traced by point P.
workingSteps: The locus of points equidistant from a line (directrix) and a point (focus) is a parabola.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
The line l has equation y = −2 and the fixed point is F(0, 2).
Show that the equation of the curve is y = x²/8.

markScheme.desc: Distance from P(x, y) to line y = −2 is y + 2
Distance from P(x, y) to F(0, 2) is √(x² + (y−2)²)

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Write down the coordinates of the vertex of this parabola.
workingSteps: y = x²/8 has its minimum at x = 0, y = 0.
